---
title: "FST_outlier_analysis"
author: "Elizabeth Campbell"
date: "2026-02-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#===============================================================================
# FST OUTLIER ANALYSIS FOR CLIMATE-ASSOCIATED SNPs
# 
# Analysis of Population Differentiation at Climate Adaptation Loci
# in Intermediate Wheatgrass (Thinopyrum intermedium)
#
# Author: Elizabeth Campbell-Strupp
# Affiliation: Department of Tropical Plant and Soil Sciences,
#              University of Hawaii at Manoa
# Date: 2/3/2026
#
# Description: Calculates FST for climate-associated SNPs to identify signatures
#              of divergent selection across six genetic populations. Uses Weir 
#              and Cockerham's (1984) method to quantify population differentiation.
#
# References: 
#   - Weir & Cockerham (1984) Evolution 38:1358-1370
#   - Holsinger & Weir (2009) Nat Rev Genet 10:639-650
#===============================================================================

#===============================================================================
# 1. SETUP AND CONFIGURATION
#===============================================================================

# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)          # Data manipulation
library(tidyr)          # Data tidying
library(ggplot2)        # Publication-quality graphics
library(scales)         # Plot scaling functions

# Set working directory - MODIFY THIS PATH FOR YOUR SYSTEM
BASE_DIR <- "path/to/your/data"
setwd(BASE_DIR)

# Input file paths
GENOTYPE_FILE <- "IWG_numeric_genotype.txt"         # Numeric genotype matrix (0/1/2)
PCA_CLUSTERS_FILE <- "IWG_pca_with_clusters.csv"    # Population assignments (K=6)
SIGNIFICANT_SNPS_FILE <- "GWAS_significant_SNPs_Q+K_model.csv"  # Climate-associated SNPs

# Output directory
OUTPUT_DIR <- "FST_Analysis"
if (!dir.exists(OUTPUT_DIR)) dir.create(OUTPUT_DIR)

# FST classification thresholds (Holsinger & Weir, 2009)
THRESHOLD_HIGH <- 0.15        # High differentiation
THRESHOLD_EXTREME <- 0.35     # Extreme outlier (strong divergent selection)

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("FST OUTLIER ANALYSIS FOR CLIMATE-ASSOCIATED SNPs\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

#===============================================================================
# 2. DATA LOADING AND PREPARATION
#===============================================================================

cat("STEP 1: Loading data...\n")
cat("--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n")

# Load genotype matrix (SNPs × samples)
# Format: Rows = SNPs, Columns = samples, Values = 0/1/2 (allele dosage)
genotype <- read.table(GENOTYPE_FILE, 
                       header = TRUE, 
                       row.names = 1, 
                       check.names = FALSE)

# Load population assignments from HCPC clustering
pca_clusters <- read.csv(PCA_CLUSTERS_FILE)

# Load significant SNPs from GWAS (Q+K model)
sig_snps <- read.csv(SIGNIFICANT_SNPS_FILE)

# Extract unique marker names from significant SNPs
sig_markers <- unique(sig_snps$marker)
cat(sprintf("Number of climate-associated SNPs: %d\n", length(sig_markers)))
cat(sprintf("Number of populations (K): %d\n", length(unique(pca_clusters$clust))))
cat(sprintf("Total genotyped samples: %d\n\n", ncol(genotype)))

# Filter genotype matrix to significant SNPs only
genotype_sig <- genotype[sig_markers, ]

# Ensure sample names match between genotype and population data
# Column name in pca_clusters should be adjusted based on your file
pop_sample_col <- "sample.id"  # Adjust if different in your file
cluster_col <- "clust"          # Adjust if different in your file

common_samples <- intersect(colnames(genotype_sig), pca_clusters[[pop_sample_col]])
genotype_sig <- genotype_sig[, common_samples]
pca_clusters_filtered <- pca_clusters[pca_clusters[[pop_sample_col]] %in% common_samples, ]

cat(sprintf("Samples with both genotype and population data: %d\n", length(common_samples)))
cat(sprintf("SNPs retained for analysis: %d\n\n", nrow(genotype_sig)))

# Display population sizes
pop_sizes <- table(pca_clusters_filtered[[cluster_col]])
cat("Population sizes:\n")
for (i in 1:length(pop_sizes)) {
  cat(sprintf("  Population %d: n = %d\n", i, pop_sizes[i]))
}
cat("\n")

#===============================================================================
# 3. CALCULATE ALLELE FREQUENCIES BY POPULATION
#===============================================================================

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 2: Calculating allele frequencies by population\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

# Initialize results dataframe
allele_freq_by_pop <- data.frame()

# Loop through each significant SNP
for (marker in sig_markers) {
  
  # Extract genotype vector for this marker (0/1/2 coding)
  marker_geno <- as.numeric(genotype_sig[marker, ])
  
  # Calculate allele frequencies for each population
  for (pop in sort(unique(pca_clusters_filtered[[cluster_col]]))) {
    
    # Identify samples in this population
    pop_sample_ids <- pca_clusters_filtered[[pop_sample_col]][
      pca_clusters_filtered[[cluster_col]] == pop
    ]
    pop_indices <- which(colnames(genotype_sig) %in% pop_sample_ids)
    
    # Extract genotypes for this population
    pop_geno <- marker_geno[pop_indices]
    pop_geno_clean <- pop_geno[!is.na(pop_geno)]
    
    # Skip if no data for this population
    if (length(pop_geno_clean) == 0) next
    
    # Calculate allele frequency
    # In 0/1/2 coding: 0 = ref/ref, 1 = ref/alt, 2 = alt/alt
    n_samples <- length(pop_geno_clean)
    n_alleles <- n_samples * 2  # Diploid
    alt_allele_count <- sum(pop_geno_clean)  # Total alternate alleles
    alt_allele_freq <- alt_allele_count / n_alleles
    
    # Store results
    allele_freq_by_pop <- rbind(allele_freq_by_pop, data.frame(
      marker = marker,
      population = paste0("Pop_", pop),
      n_samples = n_samples,
      n_alleles = n_alleles,
      alt_count = alt_allele_count,
      alt_freq = alt_allele_freq,
      ref_freq = 1 - alt_allele_freq
    ))
  }
}

# Summary statistics
cat("Allele frequency calculation complete\n\n")
cat("Summary by population:\n")
freq_summary <- allele_freq_by_pop %>%
  group_by(population) %>%
  summarise(
    n_samples = first(n_samples),
    mean_alt_freq = mean(alt_freq),
    min_alt_freq = min(alt_freq),
    max_alt_freq = max(alt_freq),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(population)

for (i in 1:nrow(freq_summary)) {
  cat(sprintf("  %s: n = %d, mean freq = %.3f (range: %.3f - %.3f)\n",
              freq_summary$population[i],
              freq_summary$n_samples[i],
              freq_summary$mean_alt_freq[i],
              freq_summary$min_alt_freq[i],
              freq_summary$max_alt_freq[i]))
}
cat("\n")

#===============================================================================
# 4. CALCULATE FST USING WEIR & COCKERHAM (1984)
#===============================================================================

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 3: Calculating FST (Weir & Cockerham 1984)\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

cat("Method: FST = (HT - HS) / HT\n")
cat("  HT = Total expected heterozygosity (2p(1-p) using global frequency)\n")
cat("  HS = Average expected heterozygosity within populations\n\n")

# Initialize results dataframe
fst_results <- data.frame()

# Calculate FST for each marker
for (marker in unique(allele_freq_by_pop$marker)) {
  
  # Extract data for this marker across all populations
  marker_data <- allele_freq_by_pop %>%
    filter(marker == !!marker)
  
  # Need at least 2 populations
  if (nrow(marker_data) < 2) next
  
  # Calculate global allele frequency (across all populations)
  total_alt_alleles <- sum(marker_data$alt_count)
  total_alleles <- sum(marker_data$n_alleles)
  p_global <- total_alt_alleles / total_alleles  # Global alternate allele frequency
  
  n_pops <- nrow(marker_data)
  
  # HT: Total expected heterozygosity using global frequency
  # Formula: HT = 2 * p * (1 - p)
  # This represents expected heterozygosity if all populations were panmictic
  H_T <- 2 * p_global * (1 - p_global)
  
  # HS: Average expected heterozygosity within populations
  # Average of 2 * p_i * (1 - p_i) across all populations
  H_S <- 0
  for (i in 1:nrow(marker_data)) {
    p_i <- marker_data$alt_freq[i]  # Population-specific frequency
    H_S <- H_S + (2 * p_i * (1 - p_i))
  }
  H_S <- H_S / n_pops
  
  # Calculate FST
  # FST = (HT - HS) / HT
  # Interpretation: Proportion of total genetic variance due to population structure
  if (H_T > 0) {
    fst <- (H_T - H_S) / H_T
  } else {
    fst <- 0  # No variation at this locus
  }
  
  # Calculate variance in allele frequencies across populations
  # Higher variance indicates stronger population differentiation
  freq_variance <- var(marker_data$alt_freq)
  freq_range <- max(marker_data$alt_freq) - min(marker_data$alt_freq)
  
  # Store results
  fst_results <- rbind(fst_results, data.frame(
    marker = marker,
    FST = fst,
    global_freq = p_global,
    H_T = H_T,
    H_S = H_S,
    freq_variance = freq_variance,
    freq_range = freq_range,
    n_pops = n_pops
  ))
}

# Sort by FST (highest to lowest)
fst_results <- fst_results %>%
  arrange(desc(FST))

cat(sprintf("FST calculated for %d SNPs\n\n", nrow(fst_results)))

#===============================================================================
# 5. CLASSIFY OUTLIERS BASED ON FST THRESHOLDS
#===============================================================================

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 4: FST classification (Holsinger & Weir 2009)\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

# Calculate summary statistics
fst_mean <- mean(fst_results$FST)
fst_median <- median(fst_results$FST)
fst_sd <- sd(fst_results$FST)
fst_min <- min(fst_results$FST)
fst_max <- max(fst_results$FST)

cat("FST distribution summary:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Mean:   %.3f\n", fst_mean))
cat(sprintf("  Median: %.3f\n", fst_median))
cat(sprintf("  SD:     %.3f\n", fst_sd))
cat(sprintf("  Range:  %.3f - %.3f\n\n", fst_min, fst_max))

# Classify SNPs based on FST thresholds
fst_results <- fst_results %>%
  mutate(
    classification = case_when(
      FST > THRESHOLD_EXTREME ~ "Extreme outlier",
      FST > THRESHOLD_HIGH ~ "High differentiation",
      TRUE ~ "Normal"
    ),
    interpretation = case_when(
      FST > THRESHOLD_EXTREME ~ "Strong divergent selection",
      FST > THRESHOLD_HIGH ~ "Moderate divergent selection",
      TRUE ~ "Parallel adaptation"
    )
  )

# Summary by classification
cat("Classification results:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Threshold for high differentiation: FST > %.2f\n", THRESHOLD_HIGH))
cat(sprintf("  Threshold for extreme outliers: FST > %.2f\n\n", THRESHOLD_EXTREME))

classification_summary <- fst_results %>%
  group_by(classification, interpretation) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    percentage = n() / nrow(fst_results) * 100,
    mean_fst = mean(FST),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_fst))

for (i in 1:nrow(classification_summary)) {
  cat(sprintf("  %s (FST = %.3f):\n", 
              classification_summary$classification[i],
              classification_summary$mean_fst[i]))
  cat(sprintf("    %d SNPs (%.1f%%)\n",
              classification_summary$n[i],
              classification_summary$percentage[i]))
  cat(sprintf("    Interpretation: %s\n\n",
              classification_summary$interpretation[i]))
}

# Display top FST outliers
cat("Top 10 FST outliers:\n")
top_outliers <- fst_results %>%
  arrange(desc(FST)) %>%
  head(10)

for (i in 1:nrow(top_outliers)) {
  cat(sprintf("  %2d. %-20s FST = %.3f  [%s]\n",
              i,
              top_outliers$marker[i],
              top_outliers$FST[i],
              top_outliers$classification[i]))
}
cat("\n")

#===============================================================================
# 6. BIOLOGICAL INTERPRETATION
#===============================================================================

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("BIOLOGICAL INTERPRETATION\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

n_extreme <- sum(fst_results$classification == "Extreme outlier")
n_high <- sum(fst_results$classification == "High differentiation")
n_normal <- sum(fst_results$classification == "Normal")

pct_divergent <- (n_extreme + n_high) / nrow(fst_results) * 100

cat("Overall interpretation:\n\n")

if (fst_mean < 0.05) {
  cat("  Overall FST < 0.05: LOW population differentiation\n")
  cat("  → Populations are genetically similar at climate-associated loci\n")
  cat("  → Adaptation through shared standing genetic variation\n")
  cat("  → Parallel evolution across climatic gradients\n\n")
} else if (fst_mean < 0.15) {
  cat("  Overall FST = 0.05-0.15: MODERATE population differentiation\n")
  cat("  → Some population structure at climate-associated loci\n")
  cat("  → Mix of parallel and divergent adaptation\n\n")
} else {
  cat("  Overall FST > 0.15: HIGH population differentiation\n")
  cat("  → Strong population structure at climate-associated loci\n")
  cat("  → Predominantly divergent selection across populations\n\n")
}

cat(sprintf("Divergent selection signature: %.1f%% of climate-associated SNPs\n", 
            pct_divergent))
cat(sprintf("  - Extreme outliers (FST > 0.35): %d SNPs (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_extreme, n_extreme/nrow(fst_results)*100))
cat(sprintf("    → Strong divergent selection (population-specific adaptations)\n\n"))
cat(sprintf("  - High differentiation (FST > 0.15): %d SNPs (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_high, n_high/nrow(fst_results)*100))
cat(sprintf("    → Moderate divergent selection (regional adaptations)\n\n"))
cat(sprintf("  - Normal differentiation (FST ≤ 0.15): %d SNPs (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_normal, n_normal/nrow(fst_results)*100))
cat(sprintf("    → Parallel adaptation (shared standing variation)\n\n"))

#===============================================================================
# 7. EXPORT RESULTS
#===============================================================================

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("EXPORTING RESULTS\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

# Export FST results
fst_output <- fst_results %>%
  select(marker, FST, classification, interpretation, 
         global_freq, freq_variance, freq_range, H_T, H_S)

write.csv(fst_output, 
          file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "FST_results_climate_SNPs.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

# Export allele frequencies
write.csv(allele_freq_by_pop,
          file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "Allele_frequencies_by_population.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

# Export summary table for manuscript
manuscript_summary <- fst_results %>%
  filter(FST > THRESHOLD_HIGH) %>%  # Only outliers
  arrange(desc(FST)) %>%
  select(marker, FST, classification, global_freq, freq_range)

write.csv(manuscript_summary,
          file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "FST_outliers_for_manuscript.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("Results exported:\n")
cat(sprintf("  1. %s/FST_results_climate_SNPs.csv\n", OUTPUT_DIR))
cat(sprintf("  2. %s/Allele_frequencies_by_population.csv\n", OUTPUT_DIR))
cat(sprintf("  3. %s/FST_outliers_for_manuscript.csv\n", OUTPUT_DIR))
cat("\n")

#===============================================================================
# 8. VISUALIZATION
#===============================================================================

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("CREATING PUBLICATION-QUALITY FIGURES\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

# Set color palette for classifications
classification_colors <- c(
  "Extreme outlier" = "#d62728",        # Red
  "High differentiation" = "#ff7f0e",   # Orange
  "Normal" = "#7f7f7f"                  # Gray
)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Figure 1: FST Distribution Histogram
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

p1 <- ggplot(fst_results, aes(x = FST, fill = classification)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = THRESHOLD_HIGH, 
             linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "#ff7f0e", 
             linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = THRESHOLD_EXTREME, 
             linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "#d62728", 
             linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = THRESHOLD_HIGH, y = Inf, 
           label = paste0("FST = ", THRESHOLD_HIGH), 
           vjust = 1.5, hjust = 1.1, size = 3.5, color = "#ff7f0e") +
  annotate("text", x = THRESHOLD_EXTREME, y = Inf, 
           label = paste0("FST = ", THRESHOLD_EXTREME), 
           vjust = 1.5, hjust = 1.1, size = 3.5, color = "#d62728") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = classification_colors,
                    name = "Classification") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of FST at Climate-Associated Loci",
    subtitle = sprintf("n = %d SNPs across %d populations", 
                      nrow(fst_results), 
                      length(unique(pca_clusters_filtered[[cluster_col]]))),
    x = expression(paste("F"[ST])),
    y = "Number of SNPs"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")
  )

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "Figure_FST_distribution.pdf"), 
       p1, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "Figure_FST_distribution.png"), 
       p1, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Figure 2: FST vs Global Allele Frequency
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

p2 <- ggplot(fst_results, aes(x = global_freq, y = FST, 
                               color = classification,
                               shape = classification)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = THRESHOLD_HIGH, 
             linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "#ff7f0e",
             linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = THRESHOLD_EXTREME, 
             linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "#d62728",
             linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = classification_colors,
                     name = "Classification") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 15),
                     name = "Classification") +
  labs(
    title = expression(paste("F"[ST], " vs. Global Allele Frequency")),
    subtitle = "Climate-associated SNPs across six genetic populations",
    x = "Global Allele Frequency",
    y = expression(paste("F"[ST]))
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")
  )

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "Figure_FST_vs_frequency.pdf"), 
       p2, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "Figure_FST_vs_frequency.png"), 
       p2, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Figure 3: Allele Frequency Heatmap (Top Outliers)
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Select top 15 FST outliers for visualization
top_n_markers <- 15
top_markers <- fst_results %>%
  arrange(desc(FST)) %>%
  head(top_n_markers) %>%
  pull(marker)

# Prepare data for heatmap
heatmap_data <- allele_freq_by_pop %>%
  filter(marker %in% top_markers) %>%
  mutate(marker = factor(marker, levels = top_markers))

p3 <- ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = population, y = marker, fill = alt_freq)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", alt_freq)), 
            size = 3, color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2166ac", mid = "white", high = "#b2182b",
                       midpoint = 0.5,
                       name = "Allele\nFrequency") +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Allele Frequencies at Top ", top_n_markers, " FST Outliers"),
    subtitle = "Across six genetic populations",
    x = "Population",
    y = "SNP"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "Figure_FST_allele_freq_heatmap.pdf"), 
       p3, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "Figure_FST_allele_freq_heatmap.png"), 
       p3, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

cat("Figures exported:\n")
cat(sprintf("  1. %s/Figure_FST_distribution.pdf\n", OUTPUT_DIR))
cat(sprintf("  2. %s/Figure_FST_vs_frequency.pdf\n", OUTPUT_DIR))
cat(sprintf("  3. %s/Figure_FST_allele_freq_heatmap.pdf\n", OUTPUT_DIR))
cat("\n")

#===============================================================================
# 9. SUMMARY STATISTICS FOR MANUSCRIPT
#===============================================================================

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("SUMMARY FOR MANUSCRIPT\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

cat("Key findings:\n\n")

cat(sprintf("1. FST distribution:\n"))
cat(sprintf("   - Mean FST = %.3f ± %.3f (SD)\n", fst_mean, fst_sd))
cat(sprintf("   - Range: %.3f - %.3f\n\n", fst_min, fst_max))

cat(sprintf("2. Classification of %d climate-associated SNPs:\n", nrow(fst_results)))
cat(sprintf("   - Extreme outliers (FST > 0.35): %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_extreme, n_extreme/nrow(fst_results)*100))
cat(sprintf("   - High differentiation (FST > 0.15): %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_high, n_high/nrow(fst_results)*100))
cat(sprintf("   - Normal differentiation (FST ≤ 0.15): %d (%.1f%%)\n\n",
            n_normal, n_normal/nrow(fst_results)*100))

cat(sprintf("3. Biological interpretation:\n"))
if (pct_divergent > 50) {
  cat(sprintf("   - Predominantly divergent selection (%.1f%% with FST > 0.15)\n", 
              pct_divergent))
  cat("   - Populations have adapted to local climates through different alleles\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("   - Predominantly parallel adaptation (%.1f%% with FST ≤ 0.15)\n",
              100 - pct_divergent))
  cat("   - Populations share adaptive alleles (standing genetic variation)\n")
}

cat("\n================================================================================\n")
cat("FST OUTLIER ANALYSIS COMPLETE\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

cat("All results have been saved to:", OUTPUT_DIR, "\n\n")

#===============================================================================
# END OF SCRIPT
#===============================================================================
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
